# PAGE CONTEXT: homepage.md

## Page Voice Note

This is the first impression for three distinct visitor personas coming from three different mental states:
- **Legacy Moderniser:** "My website is outdated and doesn't reflect my credibility."
- **Burned Decision Maker:** "I've wasted money on agency work before."
- **Strategically Unaware:** "I don't know what I need."

**Voice calibration:** Maximum authority, minimum jargon. Lead with the clearest positioning claims. Every sentence must earn its place. Visitors will spend less than 10 seconds deciding whether to scroll. The Hero must surface the core tension WebXC resolves: the gap between what the business IS (expertise, track record, proven results) and what its digital presence SAYS it is (generic, outdated, underperforming). Use positive contrast — emphasise what becomes possible without dwelling on pain. Tone: calm, confident, peer-to-peer. This is not a salesperson. This is a business strategist who has solved this problem hundreds of times.

## Relevant Source Files

- Service-Offerings.md (defines Options A, B, discovery, ongoing programme, benefits delivered)
- Positioning.md (defines positioning framework, value themes, audience personas)
- Key Messaging.md (defines benefit messages, proofs, core message)
- Writer Prompt (brand voice, trust signals, narrative rules)
- Writing Style Guide (sentence rhythm, bilingual voice, hard rules)

## Critical Facts & Details

From Service-Offerings.md:
- Three entry paths: Discovery & Strategic Review (paid diagnostic), Option A (Positioning & Digital Authority Programme), Option B (Complete Digital Foundation Build)
- Every engagement begins with a conversation (free fit check)
- Discovery delivers: positioning review, website assessment, AI visibility baseline, competitor comparison, search intent mapping, entity signal assessment, prioritised recommendations
- Option A: Business positioning, service taxonomy, content architecture, personal brand/entity alignment, semantic/structural improvements, off-site recommendations
- Option B: Option A + visual design system + CMS implementation on WordPress + conversion pathway design + 90+ PageSpeed targeting + Core Web Vitals optimisation + AI crawler optimisation
- Ongoing Programme: Content strategy, authority monitoring, strategic advisory (quarterly reviews)

From Positioning.md:
- Value Proposition: "Your expertise made visible, citable, and converting."
- Four core themes: (1) Business Strategy Before Technical Work, (2) Conversion & Performance Machine, (3) AI & Search Readiness, (4) Owned Digital Asset
- Target audiences: Legacy Moderniser, Burned Decision Maker, Strategically Unaware Leader, Ethical Medical/YMYL Specialist
- Principal-led work differentiator (not delegated to juniors)
- 90+ PageSpeed scores as performance standard
- Dual-channel visibility (Google + AI systems)

From Key Messaging.md:
- Benefit 1: "Your website starts converting like your best salesperson" — Proof: 90+ PageSpeed targets, Core Web Vitals, conversion pathway design
- Benefit 2: "Become discoverable and citable by AI — not just Google" — Proof: Schema markup, entity resolution, personal brand alignment
- Benefit 3: "Every dollar builds an asset you permanently own" — Proof: 100% client ownership, no opaque retainers

## Section Narrative Plan

| Section | Narrative Role | Opens With (Conflict) | Resolves With | Key New Evidence | Frame Element | Transition From Previous | Transition To Next |
|---------|---------------|----------------------|---------------|-----------------|---------------|-------------------------|-------------------|
| Global Header | Navigation anchor | n/a — persistent | n/a | Trust signal: CTA visible | Neutral facilitator | n/a | Seamless to Hero |
| Hero | Hook + authority signal | Gap between what business IS and what site SAYS (positive contrast) | Value proposition: "Your expertise made visible, citable, and converting" + CTA | Principal-led difference, target audience signal ($2M–$50M businesses) | WebXC as peer guide; visitor recognises gap | n/a — first section | Immediate routing options below |
| Rapid Qualification | Router by scenario | Three visitor personas = three paths | Conditional routing to next relevant page | Acknowledgment that WebXC matches different readiness states | Neutral facilitator; visitor self-identifies | Hero closing → "Which describes your situation?" | One of three directions based on scenario selection |
| Services Overview | Solution menu + differentiation | Problems: outdated sites, generic positioning, slow performance, invisibility to AI | Three service pathways, each solving different readiness level | Three distinct options with clear value prop per card, ownership positioning | WebXC as menu of solutions; visitor chooses fit | "We have three paths..." from qualification | Shift to proof/credibility (The Principals) |
| The Principals | Proof + credibility | Why principal-led matters (implicit contrast to delegated/junior work) | "We bring [X] years combined. We do the strategic work. You're not handed to juniors." | Forty years combined, visible principals, specific expertise domains | Authority figures; trusted guides with skin in the game | "Here's who leads the work" | Shift to outcomes/impact |
| CTA Strip | Momentum capture | Visitor has context, ready to move | Specific CTA action (Book conversation) | Action is named: "Book Your Strategic Review" or "Start Your Consultation" | Direct call-to-action bridge | "Now you know who we are" | Transition to proof (social/trust) |
| Social Proof | Trust stacking | Why believe this works? (implicit doubt) | Case studies, results, outcomes, specificity | Real outcomes: conversion improvements, speed metrics, clients served | Evidence layer; specific proof | "Here's what this approach delivers" | Transition to detail/FAQ |
| FAQ | Objection handling | Visitor questions/hesitations (What's different? How is this not like other agencies? What happens if I'm unsure?) | Clear, specific answers grounded in methodology | Specific methodology answers, no jargon, no hedging | Educational clarity; removing friction | "Questions?" or "Here's what you need to know" | Transition to final CTA |
| Trust Signals | Credibility reinforcement | Generic websites don't inspire confidence (implicit) | Credentials, certifications, affiliations, security signals, reviews aggregate | Specific trust markers: team credentials, security implementation, review aggregates | Authority signals; institutional credibility | "Before you decide..." | Transition to final CTA |
| Call to Action | Conversion funnel | Visitor has all info, now what? (implicit: don't lose momentum) | Clear, single primary CTA + secondary options | Named action steps: "Book Your Strategic Review" + secondary CTAs (Take Self-Assessment, Explore Services) | Direct invitation; low-friction decision | "You now know who we are and what we do" | None (or Footer) |
| Footer | Navigation persistence | n/a — structural | n/a | Sitemap, secondary links, legal, contact info | Neutral facilitator | Page-level closures | n/a — page end |

## Query Vocabulary Alignment

| Search Term | Brand Term | Usage Rule |
|------------|-----------|-----------|
| "Website rebuild" | "Complete Digital Foundation Build" / "Option B" | Use "website rebuild" in Hero H1 or early body, brand term in service cards |
| "Web design agency NZ" | "Principal-led digital performance partner" | Brand term in Hero value prop |
| "Conversion-focused web design" | "Conversion-engineered architecture" | Use search term in H2, brand term in body copy |
| "SEO agency NZ" | "Semantic architecture" / "AI & search readiness" | Avoid direct SEO language; use "AI visibility" and "search readiness" |
| "WordPress web design" | "CMS implementation" | Technical detail for later sections; avoid in Hero |
| "Established business websites" | "Digital foundation for $2M–$50M businesses" | Use search term concept in qualification section |

| Call to Action | Commitment & clarity | partnership, review, conversation, clarity, transformation |

## Context Depth by Section

| Section | Depth Level | Implication for Copy |
|---------|-----------|---------------------|
| Hero | BROAD | One clear value statement + one supporting line. No detail. Maximum clarity, minimum complexity. 80-120 words. |
| Rapid Qualification | BROAD | Three scenario cards, each 40-60 words. Quick recognition, fast decision. |
| Services Overview | BROAD | Three service cards with equivalent weight. Each 80-100 words. Lead with benefit, support with differentiator, close with CTA. |
| The Principals | BROAD | Two-line credibility signal + brief bio anchor. 100-150 words total. Save depth for About page. |
| CTA Strip | BROAD | 60-80 words. Single call-to-action with secondary options. Maximum clarity, no hedging. |
| Social Proof | BROAD | 3-4 proof points, each 40-60 words. Specific metrics, real outcomes. |
| FAQ | BROAD to MODERATE | 5-7 most common objections. Each 60-100 words. Specific answers, no fluff. |
| Trust Signals | BROAD | Credential list + security marker + review aggregate. 100-150 words max. |
| Call to Action | BROAD | Final push. 60-80 words. Single primary CTA + secondary options. |

## SEO Distribution Plan

**Homepage Strategy:**
- Primary keyword (head term): "Digital foundation" or "website rebuild" — appears once in Hero H1, referenced naturally in Services Overview
- Entity clustering: WebXC (organisation), James & Andy (people), Principal-led work (methodology) all clustered in Principals section
- Location: NZ/established business audience signalled in Hero, repeated in qualification scenarios
- Semantic depth: Balanced across sections — no single section overloaded with keywords
- Thematic fields well-distributed: positioning (Hero), service architecture (Services Overview), leadership (Principals), clarity (FAQ)

**Anti-stuffing applied:**
- "90+ PageSpeed" appears once (Services Overview) + brief reference in FAQ if needed
- "Principal-led" appears in Hero + The Principals section + once more in FAQ (not stuffed)
- Service names (Option A, Option B) appear once per card in Services Overview
- "Owned asset" appears once prominently in Services Overview or CTA
